feed.title: SAP Community - API Management
feed.subtitle: API Management Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: API Management is not working for me
entry.content:
When i am trying to create API Management, developer portal in SAP Trail account, i am unable to create it it giving error as 'Creation Failed' and when i try to delete the Failed API its giving 'Deletion Failed'.

Need Help...!

entry.title: How to call SAP ByDesign API from S4HANA Cloud System
entry.content:
I would like to call Sales Order API(ByD) from S4hana Sales Order BAdI logic.

Target is that when I save SO in S4 system, SO data from S4 will pass to ByD via API.

Is that possible to do? Is there any reference for this requirement?

Best Regards,

Khaing Zin

entry.title: How to bypass a 502 Bad Gateway - ResponseWithBody error thrown by the API Gateway?
entry.content:
Hello all,

One of our API proxies is returning an HTTP 502 Bad Gateway - ResponseWithBody response. This is due to the API Gateway considering the response of the API provider incorrect, as it contains an "Content-Encoding" header along with an HTTP 204 response code, which is not a correct HTTP response according to the RFC 7231, section 6.3.5: 204 No Content.

Because the fix on API provider side will take several months to be deployed, we would like to temporarily bypass the error and "force" the response received by the API provider being sent back to the API consumer.

We have tried different solutions without success:

  • Removing the problematic HTTP header from the response using an AssignMessage policy in the response PreFflow of the target endpoint.
  • Forcing the response code to 204 in an AssignMessage policy called from the <FaultRules> section of both the proxy and target endpoints
  • By calling the API provider using a service callout instead of a target endpoint.

Unfortunately, none of these solutions works, and the HTTP 502 Bad Gateway - ResponseWithBody is still being returned by the API proxy.

Is it possible to bypass this error and force the reponse to a specific HTTP code ?

Thanks

entry.title: API Proxy Authentication
entry.content:
Hello,

I have created API Proxy with the API provider(On-Prem) and adding policies verifykey. Problem is that when i test API Proxy with the API key then it throws an error 401 Unathorized. Please see the below Screenshots.

entry.title: How to test API proxy
entry.content:
Hello,

How do I test API proxy in the test section? What will be my basic authentication username and password? Is this an on-prem username or what?

Note :- Configured API Provider With On-prem

entry.title: API Provider With Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hello,

At the time of creating API Provider with Cloud Integration when Save and clicking on test connection then it is showing an error. How to resolve the below issue.

entry.title: API Proxy 401 Unauthorized
entry.content:
Hello,

After successfully creating API Proxy with API Provider (On-Prem) at the time of test API Proxy, it shows 401 Unauthorised. Please suggest how to resolve it, and I also want to know what my basic authentication username and password will be. It should be on-prem username & password or what?.

entry.title: SAP API Management - OAuth 2.0 authentication with SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

 

I need some help in understanding the SAML Assertion based OAuth authentication set up between SAP API Management (SAP APIM) and SuccessFactors (SF) system. I followed this blog post to set this up.

With an exception that I used "https://mySuccessFactorsTenant.com/oauth/token" end point to retrieve the OAuth token instead of using "https://mySuccessFactorsTenant.com/oauth/idp" end point, since this will be deprecated.

The idea is to not to expose the MyHr system directly to the open internet and to have security measures(OAuth 2.0 and TLS 1.2) in place.

The Proxy with this set up is working fine and we are able to connect to back-end SF system and do all operations. I have the following questions:

  1. How can I check the TLS version being used. I came across this post which says that TLS 1.2 is enforced by default. Is this also true for connection between the Consumer of the API Proxy and SAP APIM?
  2. Since the SAML assertion for OAuth with SF is being generated by a policy inside SAP APIM proxy here, can it be said that SAP APIM is idp here?
  3. New Requirements:
    1. SF wants to create dedicated Users for each Consumer to restrict access/authorizations - For now, I have implemented the logic to get the Username during runtime based on Application name and then use that Username to create Assertion/token so that authentication is done with User assigned for calling Consumer. Is this the correct way to achieve this? Any alternative way to do this?
    2. We are also required to use OAuth 2.0 for all requests coming to SAP APIM. I understand that one way to achieve this would be to have a service in SAP APIM which the consumer can call to get the OAuth token (Client credential based OAuth) as described here. This approach will result in 2 OAuth set-ups, one between Consumer <--> SAP APIM and another one between SAP APIM <--> SF.

Considering requirement from Point 3.1 and 3.2 above, what would be the recommended approach for the overall Integration i.e. to have OAuth 2.0 set up for end to end communication? Please advise.

Regards,

Faisal

entry.title: Default TLS 1.2 in SAP API Management - How to check?
entry.content:
Hello experts,

As per the below SAP post, the TLSv 1.2 has automatically been enforced in SAP API Management. So, my understanding is , any request from SAP API Management to backend system would be TLSv 1.2 secured protocol. But I would like to know, from consumer of API perspective, is it also the the same (request from consumer of the API to the SAP API Management) that the request is secured with TLSv 1.2 protocol?. Is there a way to check the TLS version in SAP API Management?. Much appreciate for any response!.

Limits in API Management 

Thanks,

Azeem

entry.title: How to perform POST using the Attachment API in Business Accelerator Hub Try Out feature
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I want to achieve this scenario: Maintenance Order already exists in the system and API Attachment is used to upload an attachment to S/4HANA Cloud, assign it to and create a DIR, then assign the DIR to the Maintenance Order.

We want to perform the POST operation using the Attachment API in Business Accelerator Hub Try Out feature since we were able to connect our Test system and was able to perform the GET operation.

Can you help us how to test that?

The POST function is under: Document Management > /AttachmentContentSet > Uploads attachment to a business object

See reference blog below that is technically it is possible but was on different method: Maintain Assignment of Document Info Record to Mai... - SAP Community

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: API Enterprise Project using SAC and S/4 Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear experts,

We have successsfully tested the Enterprise Project API, using the Postman application. With Postman we have simulated this API and were able to create na enterprise project and its WBS Elements.

This is the API link that we've configured to simulate this connection: https://my412440-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_ENTERPRISE_PROJECT_SRV;v=0002/A_EnterpriseProject

To use the API we have created a communication user (TESTEAPIPROJ), a communication system (SAC_S4HANA_PROJ) and the communication arrangement (SAP_COM_0308). We have attached screenshot of the mentioned objects in the document "API Enterprise Project using SAC and S4 Public Cloud.pdf".

The final step would be to connect SAC to the API so that we can create an enterprise project in the S/4HANA Public Cloud system from SAP Analytics Cloud.

In SAC we created a connection (testeodata) with basic authentication type. The user associated to this connection is the communication user that we have used previously in this process. Also, in SAC, we cannot copy the full API link. The system only accepts until https://my412440-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_ENTERPRISE_PROJECT_SRV;v=0002

However, in this system, we are not able to specify the method to call the previously mentioned API.

How should we proceed to connect both systems using this API?

Best regards,

Francisco

entry.title: API Management vs Open Connectors
entry.content:
Technology Q&A

Dear all, as a background I am working on a project where we need to consume some API's and make them available in BTP. We had a meeting with the customers and as a solution we have presented them Integration Flows and API Management. We are not having experience with Integration Flows and API Management so we are currently on a learning curve and trying to learn more about them.

While initiating the project they wanted us to configure Open Connectors(on top of Integration Flows) in order to make their APIs externally visible and be able to be commercialized. 
When I did some research we found that the best option for this purpose was the API Management on top of Integration Flows - as I understood Open Connectors might not be available anymore in the future.

Now the customer is always asking what's the difference between them Open Connectors vs API Management. Can someone please help me to differentiate API Management from Open Connectors and show the advantages/disadvantages ?

Their goal is to have their APIs exposed to a marketplace, have external visibility and in future to be able to commercialize them...Can we help them reach their goal with API Management? Is there a place where the APIs can be exposed in the same way as Open Connectors?
Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Authentication using JWT in SAP API Management
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are in a process of implementing OAuth(client credentials based) authentication to API proxies using the JWT verify policy in SAP API Management as described in this post. We used EntraID as IdP. We have been able to successfully implement and test it, but have an observation though. 

We noticed during testing that token fetched for app registered for Dev environment is also working for authenticating with API proxy in SAP APIM Test environment, which is most likely because both the APIs (dev and test) are registered in a common EntraID. 
But is this how it should work? Is there a way to ensure that token fetched for API in one environment does not works for APIs in another environment?

Any inputs will be appreciated. Thanks!

Regards,

Faisal

entry.title: SAP API Management - Set Basic Authentication for API Proxy in Integration Suite
entry.content:
Dear All,

In SAP Integration Suite, Basic Authentication offers a simple way to safeguard your API proxies using a username and password. This blog covers how to configure Basic Authentication for your API proxy in API Management.

Follow the Below Steps to Set Basic Authentication to your API Proxy.

Step 1 : Create a Proxy with your target endpoint (For test purpose i used httpbin URL)

https://httpbin.org/get

 

 



Step 2 : Go to Policies and Assign Basic Authentication in PreFlow Incoming request Stream

 

 Provide the below code in the policy editor:

 

<BasicAuthentication async='true' continueOnError='false' enabled='true' xmlns='http://www.sap.com/apimgmt'>
	<Operation>Decode</Operation>
	<IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>true</IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>
	<User ref='current.username'></User>
	<Password ref='current.password'></Password>
	<Source>request.header.Authorization</Source>
</BasicAuthentication>

 

 

Step 3 : Add Raise Fault Policy in PreFlow Incoming Stream (Next to the Basic Authentication Policy)

 

 

<RaiseFault async="true" continueOnError="false" enabled="true" xmlns="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt">
    <FaultResponse>
        <Set>
            <Headers/>
            <Payload contentType="application/json">{"status" : "Error", "messege" : "401 Unauthorized" } </Payload>
            <StatusCode>401</StatusCode>
            <ReasonPhrase>Unauthorized</ReasonPhrase>
        </Set>
    </FaultResponse>
    <IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>true</IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>
</RaiseFault>

 

In RaiseFault Policy ConditionString Provide below Code (Replace "YOUR_USERNAME" & "YOUR_PASSWORD" with your credentials)

 

(current.username != "YOUR_USERNAME")  OR (current.password != "YOUR_PASSWORD")

 

Save and Deploy.

Testing : 

To Test the Basic Authentication Try a GET Call for Your API Proxy in PostMan:

Positive Testing : 

Provide the exact Username and Password given in the Condition String :


Negative Testing : 

Provide an incorrect username and password to verify if access is correctly denied.

Incorrect Username :

Incorrect Password :

Thank You!

entry.title: SAP CPI open connection error with google ads
entry.content: Hi I am trying to connect with SAP CPI with Integration suite to google ads API. I have made all the the required steps in google developer console. But while creating instance i am getting error as "Failed to authenticate instance". Can somebody guide me what i am doing wrong ?

entry.title: SAP CPI open connection error "Failed to authenticate instance" with google ads
entry.content: I am trying to connect SAP CPI with google ADS but getting error as "Failed to authenticate instance".  Please let me know if somebody have any idea how to solve this error

entry.title: Use Of Key value map associated in API Proxy
entry.content:
Hi, 

Do you know what is the use of this section in the API proxy? I am using some Key value maps in the policy and I dont see any info under Key value map assocaited in the API proxy page

Were you able to see some entries ? How to bring the entries here when API proxy is using key value map?

 

Regards,

Muni

entry.title: How to create attachments using API_CV_ATTACHMENT?
entry.content: I need to create to create attachments, from CPI, to a Purchase Contract document using the API API_CV_ATTACHMENT. Does someone already did this?

entry.title: Http_Communication_Failure in IF_HTTP_CLIENT-->receive
entry.content:
Hi experts,
Im consumin an api in my abap code. but it resulting in dump "http communicaiton failure". while i debugged the program i found it gives dump at receive methode call. need steps to resolve it asap, code is attached. thanks in advance.
TRY.
data(lv_url) = |http://******|.  " here is my url
data: o_client type ref to if_http_client.
cl_http_client=>CREATE_BY_URL( EXPORTING
                               URL = lv_url
                               IMPORTING
                                 client = o_client
                                 EXCEPTIONS
                                   ARGUMENT_NOT_FOUND = 1
                                   PLUGIN_NOT_ACTIVE = 2
                                   INTERNAL_ERROR = 3 ).
if sy-subrc <> 0.
  o_client->CLOSE( ).
  endif.
if o_client is bound.
  o_client->REQUEST->SET_METHOD( if_http_request=>CO_REQUEST_METHOD_POST ).
  o_client->REQUEST->SET_CONTENT_TYPE( 'application/json' ).

*  Set Item Details - Ended
    /UI2/CL_JSON=>SERIALIZE(
    EXPORTING
    DATA = IT_DATA        " Data to serialize
    PRETTY_NAME = /UI2/CL_JSON=>PRETTY_MODE-CAMEL_CASE
    RECEIVING
    R_JSON = GV_JSON ).   " JSON string

    o_client->REQUEST->SET_CDATA( GV_JSON ).

    o_client->SEND( timeout = if_http_client=>co_timeout_default ).
    o_client->RECEIVE( ).

    data: lv_http_Status type i,
          lv_status_text type string.

    o_client->RESPONSE->GET_STATUS(
                                   IMPORTING
                                     code = lv_http_status
                                     reason = lv_status_text ).

    write: lv_status_text.



  endif.

CATCH cx_root into data(lv_text) .

ENDTRY.

entry.title: SAP Digital Manufacturing - REST API
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I want to import data get DM REST API.

I'd like to know how I can get the full data with characters by entering only some of the GET CALL REQUEST parameter strings.

For example, when you get the tools information, toolNumber wants to get a full list with "PRT".
